    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/bootstrap/steps.go", "symbol": "DefaultConstructor.Construct"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go", "symbol": "Signature.Calculate"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go", "symbol": "Signature.ReadPluginManifestFromFS"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go", "symbol": "Signature.readPluginManifest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go", "symbol": "Signature.validateManifest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go", "symbol": "Signature.Verify"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go", "symbol": "verifyHash"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pipeline/steps.go", "symbol": "SignatureValidationStep"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pipeline/steps.go", "symbol": "SignatureValidation.Validate"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/signature.go", "symbol": "Validation.ValidateSignature"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/plugins/manager/signature/authorizer.go", "symbol": "UnsignedPluginAuthorizer.CanLoadPlugin"}
  ],
  "text": "Grafana implements plugin signature verification through a multi-layer system spanning manifest signing, signature validation on load, and unsigned plugin allowlist enforcement.\n\n## Plugin Manifest Signing and Validation\n\nThe core signature verification logic resides in `pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go`. The `Signature` struct implements the `SignatureCalculator` interface and contains:\n\n- `Calculate()`: Orchestrates the signature calculation process by reading the MANIFEST.txt file and verifying its PGP signature\n- `ReadPluginManifestFromFS()`: Loads and parses the MANIFEST.txt file from the plugin filesystem\n- `readPluginManifest()`: Decodes the PGP-signed manifest using clearsign format\n- `validateManifest()`: Validates manifest fields (plugin, version, keyId, time, files)\n- `Verify()`: Performs PGP signature verification using OpenPGP cryptography\n- `verifyHash()`: Verifies SHA256 hashes of individual plugin files against manifest entries\n\nThe `PluginManifest` struct defines manifest structure with support for both V1 (deprecated) and V2 formats, including fields for signature type, signing organization, and root URLs (for private plugins).\n\n## Signature Validation on Load\n\nPlugin signature validation during load is handled by `pkg/plugins/manager/signature/signature.go`. The `Validation` struct implements the `Validator` interface with:\n\n- `ValidateSignature()`: Main validation method that checks signature status and handles inheritance for nested plugins\n  - Returns success for valid signatures\n  - Returns success for core/internal plugins\n  - Returns success for inherited valid signatures from parent plugins\n  - Calls `CanLoadPlugin()` for unsigned plugins to check the allowlist\n  - Returns errors for invalid and modified signatures\n\n## Unsigned Plugin Allowlist Enforcement\n\nThe allowlist enforcement is implemented in `pkg/plugins/manager/signature/authorizer.go`. The `UnsignedPluginAuthorizer` struct provides:\n\n- `CanLoadPlugin()`: Authorizes plugin loading by:\n  - Allowing all non-unsigned plugins\n  - Allowing unsigned plugins in dev mode\n  - Checking if the plugin ID is in the `PluginsAllowUnsigned` configuration list\n  - Denying unsigned plugins not in the allowlist\n\n- `ProvideOSSAuthorizer()`: Factory function creating the authorizer from `PluginManagementCfg`\n\nThe allowlist configuration is loaded in `pkg/setting/setting_plugins.go` from the `[plugins]` section's `allow_loading_unsigned_plugins` setting, which accepts a comma-separated list of plugin IDs. The list is stored in `pkg/plugins/config/config.go` as `PluginsAllowUnsigned` in `PluginManagementCfg`.\n\n## Key Management\n\nPublic key retrieval for signature verification is handled through a two-tier system:\n\n1. **Static Keys** (`pkg/plugins/manager/signature/statickey/static_retriever.go`): Contains a hardcoded Grafana Labs public key used as fallback\n2. **Dynamic Keys** (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/keyretriever/dynamic/dynamic_retriever.go`): Downloads and caches keys from the Grafana.com API, updating every 10 days\n\nThe key retriever service (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/keyretriever/retriever.go`) acts as a facade, selecting between dynamic and static retrieval based on configuration.\n\n## Plugin Loading Pipeline\n\nSignature calculation and validation are integrated into the plugin loading pipeline:\n\n1. **Bootstrap Stage** (`pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/bootstrap/steps.go`): `DefaultConstructor.Construct()` calls `SignatureCalculator.Calculate()` to determine the plugin's signature status before creating the plugin object\n\n2. **Validation Stage** (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pipeline/steps.go`): `SignatureValidationStep()` creates a validation function that calls `Validation.ValidateSignature()` for each plugin\n\n3. **Configuration Integration** (`pkg/plugins/config/config.go`): `PluginManagementCfg` passes the allowlist to the `UnsignedPluginAuthorizer`\n\n## Models and Interfaces\n\nKey types are defined in `pkg/plugins/models.go`:\n- `SignatureStatus`: \"internal\", \"valid\", \"invalid\", \"modified\", \"unsigned\"\n- `SignatureType`: \"grafana\", \"commercial\", \"community\", \"private\", \"private-glob\"\n\nCore interfaces in `pkg/plugins/ifaces.go`:\n- `PluginLoaderAuthorizer`: Authorizes plugin loading\n- `SignatureCalculator`: Calculates plugin signature state\n- `KeyRetriever`: Retrieves public keys for verification\n- `KeyStore`: Stores cached public keys\n\n## API and Settings\n\nThe plugin API (`pkg/api/plugins.go`) and frontend settings (`pkg/api/frontendsettings.go`) expose plugin signature information. Wire dependency injection setup is configured in `pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginsintegration.go` and auto-generated in `pkg/server/wire_gen.go`."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Plugin Security Signature Verification

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement plugin signature verification: the plugin manifest signing, the signature validation on load, and the unsigned plugin allowlist enforcement.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Go source files implementing plugin signature verification in grafana/grafana. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for relevant code.
Now let me search for unsigned plugin allowlist and related configuration:
Let me read the key signature-related files:
Now let me search for the PluginLoaderAuthorizer implementation:
Now let me search for any additional integration files I might have missed:
Now let me create the comprehensive answer.json file with all the findings:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of all Go source files implementing plugin signature verification in Grafana. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings

I've identified **21 primary Go source files** that implement plugin signature verification across three main areas:

### 1. **Core Signature Management** (3 files)
- **manifest.go** - Reads, parses, and verifies plugin MANIFEST.txt files with PGP signatures and SHA256 hash verification
- **signature.go** - Validates signature status (valid/invalid/modified/unsigned)
- **authorizer.go** - Enforces the unsigned plugin allowlist with `CanLoadPlugin()` method

### 2. **Key Retrieval** (3 files)
- **statickey/static_retriever.go** - Hardcoded Grafana Labs public key
- **keyretriever/retriever.go** - Service provider for key retrieval
- **dynamic/dynamic_retriever.go** - Fetches and caches keys from Grafana.com API

### 3. **Plugin Loading Pipeline** (5 files)
- **bootstrap/steps.go** - Calculates signature during plugin construction
- **validation/steps.go** - Validates signatures during plugin loading
- **pipeline/steps.go** - Integration of validation into the pipeline
- Configuration and wire setup files

### 4. **Configuration & Models** (5+ files)
- Plugin models with SignatureStatus and SignatureType enums
- Configuration loading from `allow_loading_unsigned_plugins` setting
- Plugin interfaces and dependency injection setup

The complete analysis with 35 key symbols and a detailed execution chain has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json`.

arguments.query: repo:^github.com/grafana/grafana$ plugin signature manifest signing verification
repo:^github.com/grafana/grafana$ plugin load filter unsigned check ValidateSignature

  ],
  "text": "Grafana implements plugin signature verification through a multi-layer system spanning manifest signing, signature validation on load, and unsigned plugin allowlist enforcement.\n\n## Plugin Manifest Signing and Validation\n\nThe core signature verification logic resides in `pkg/plugins/manager/signature/manifest.go`. The `Signature` struct implements the `SignatureCalculator` interface and contains:\n\n- `Calculate()`: Orchestrates the signature calculation process by reading the MANIFEST.txt file and verifying its PGP signature\n- `ReadPluginManifestFromFS()`: Loads and parses the MANIFEST.txt file from the plugin filesystem\n- `readPluginManifest()`: Decodes the PGP-signed manifest using clearsign format\n- `validateManifest()`: Validates manifest fields (plugin, version, keyId, time, files)\n- `Verify()`: Performs PGP signature verification using OpenPGP cryptography\n- `verifyHash()`: Verifies SHA256 hashes of individual plugin files against manifest entries\n\nThe `PluginManifest` struct defines manifest structure with support for both V1 (deprecated) and V2 formats, including fields for signature type, signing organization, and root URLs (for private plugins).\n\n## Signature Validation on Load\n\nPlugin signature validation during load is handled by `pkg/plugins/manager/signature/signature.go`. The `Validation` struct implements the `Validator` interface with:\n\n- `ValidateSignature()`: Main validation method that checks signature status and handles inheritance for nested plugins\n  - Returns success for valid signatures\n  - Returns success for core/internal plugins\n  - Returns success for inherited valid signatures from parent plugins\n  - Calls `CanLoadPlugin()` for unsigned plugins to check the allowlist\n  - Returns errors for invalid and modified signatures\n\n## Unsigned Plugin Allowlist Enforcement\n\nThe allowlist enforcement is implemented in `pkg/plugins/manager/signature/authorizer.go`. The `UnsignedPluginAuthorizer` struct provides:\n\n- `CanLoadPlugin()`: Authorizes plugin loading by:\n  - Allowing all non-unsigned plugins\n  - Allowing unsigned plugins in dev mode\n  - Checking if the plugin ID is in the `PluginsAllowUnsigned` configuration list\n  - Denying unsigned plugins not in the allowlist\n\n- `ProvideOSSAuthorizer()`: Factory function creating the authorizer from `PluginManagementCfg`\n\nThe allowlist configuration is loaded in `pkg/setting/setting_plugins.go` from the `[plugins]` section's `allow_loading_unsigned_plugins` setting, which accepts a comma-separated list of plugin IDs. The list is stored in `pkg/plugins/config/config.go` as `PluginsAllowUnsigned` in `PluginManagementCfg`.\n\n## Key Management\n\nPublic key retrieval for signature verification is handled through a two-tier system:\n\n1. **Static Keys** (`pkg/plugins/manager/signature/statickey/static_retriever.go`): Contains a hardcoded Grafana Labs public key used as fallback\n2. **Dynamic Keys** (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/keyretriever/dynamic/dynamic_retriever.go`): Downloads and caches keys from the Grafana.com API, updating every 10 days\n\nThe key retriever service (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/keyretriever/retriever.go`) acts as a facade, selecting between dynamic and static retrieval based on configuration.\n\n## Plugin Loading Pipeline\n\nSignature calculation and validation are integrated into the plugin loading pipeline:\n\n1. **Bootstrap Stage** (`pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/bootstrap/steps.go`): `DefaultConstructor.Construct()` calls `SignatureCalculator.Calculate()` to determine the plugin's signature status before creating the plugin object\n\n2. **Validation Stage** (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pipeline/steps.go`): `SignatureValidationStep()` creates a validation function that calls `Validation.ValidateSignature()` for each plugin\n\n3. **Configuration Integration** (`pkg/plugins/config/config.go`): `PluginManagementCfg` passes the allowlist to the `UnsignedPluginAuthorizer`\n\n## Models and Interfaces\n\nKey types are defined in `pkg/plugins/models.go`:\n- `SignatureStatus`: \"internal\", \"valid\", \"invalid\", \"modified\", \"unsigned\"\n- `SignatureType`: \"grafana\", \"commercial\", \"community\", \"private\", \"private-glob\"\n\nCore interfaces in `pkg/plugins/ifaces.go`:\n- `PluginLoaderAuthorizer`: Authorizes plugin loading\n- `SignatureCalculator`: Calculates plugin signature state\n- `KeyRetriever`: Retrieves public keys for verification\n- `KeyStore`: Stores cached public keys\n\n## API and Settings\n\nThe plugin API (`pkg/api/plugins.go`) and frontend settings (`pkg/api/frontendsettings.go`) expose plugin signature information. Wire dependency injection setup is configured in `pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginsintegration.go` and auto-generated in `pkg/server/wire_gen.go`."
}
